verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "But if these are the parties, the name of member of a party is no prejudice against us.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For there are two ways, the one narrow, in which we walk; the other is for the impious, wherein they shall perish.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And yet, though the designations be alike, there is a great difference in the reality, that the way of righteousness should not be defiled by fellowship in a name. "

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: You have been afraid of the comparison of your numbers with the multitude throughout the world; and therefore, in order to win praise for the scantiness of your party, you have sought to bring in the comparison of yourself walking in the narrow path.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Would to God that you had betaken yourself not to its praise, but to the path itself!

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Truly you would have seen that there was the same scantiness in the Church of all nations; but that the righteous are said to be few in comparison with the multitude of the unrighteous, just as, in comparison with the chaff, there may be said to be few grains of corn in the most abundant crop, and yet these very grains of themselves, when brought into a heap, fill the barn.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For the followers of Maximianus themselves will surpass you in this scantiness of number, if you think that righteousness consists in this, as well as in the persecution involved in the loss of places which they held.
